NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A Song for Alamoth. God is our refuge and strength, A very present help in trouble.
NKJV.2: Therefore we will not fear, Even though the earth be removed, And though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea;
NKJV.3: Though its waters roar and be troubled, Though the mountains shake with its swelling. Selah
NKJV.4: There is a river whose streams shall make glad the city of God, The holy place of the tabernacle of the Most High.
NKJV.5: God is in the midst of her, she shall not be moved; God shall help her, just at the break of dawn.
NKJV.6: The nations raged, the kingdoms were moved; He uttered His voice, the earth melted.
NKJV.7: The LORD of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah
NKJV.8: Come, behold the works of the LORD, Who has made desolations in the earth.
NKJV.9: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth;  He breaks the bow and cuts the spear in two;  He burns the chariot in the fire.
NKJV.10: Be still, and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth!
NKJV.11: The LORD of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah

ESV.1: God is our refuge and strength, a very present  help in trouble.
ESV.2: Therefore we will not fear though the earth gives way, though the mountains be moved into the heart of the sea,
ESV.3: though its waters roar and foam, though the mountains tremble at its swelling. Selah
ESV.4: There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God, the holy habitation of the Most High.
ESV.5: God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved; God will help her when morning dawns.
ESV.6: The nations rage, the kingdoms totter; he utters his voice, the earth melts.
ESV.7: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah
ESV.8: Come, behold the works of the LORD, how he has brought desolations on the earth.
ESV.9: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth; he breaks the bow and shatters the spear; he burns the chariots with fire.
ESV.10: “Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth!”
ESV.11: The LORD of hosts is with us; the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah

NASB.1: For the choir director. [A Psalm] of the sons of Korah, set to Alamoth. A Song. God is our refuge and strength, A very present help in trouble.
NASB.2: Therefore we will not fear, though the earth should change And though the mountains slip into the heart of the sea;
NASB.3: Though its waters roar [and] foam, Though the mountains quake at its swelling pride. Selah.
NASB.4: There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God, The holy dwelling places of the Most High.
NASB.5: God is in the midst of her, she will not be moved; God will help her when morning dawns.
NASB.6: The nations made an uproar, the kingdoms tottered; He raised His voice, the earth melted.
NASB.7: The LORD of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah.
NASB.8: Come, behold the works of the LORD, Who has wrought desolations in the earth.
NASB.9: He makes wars to cease to the end of the earth; He breaks the bow and cuts the spear in two; He burns the chariots with fire.
NASB.10: 'Cease [striving] and know that I am God; I will be exalted among the nations, I will be exalted in the earth.'
NASB.11: The LORD of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our stronghold. Selah.

NLT.1:
God is our refuge and strength,
always ready to help in times of trouble.
NLT.2:
So we will not fear when earthquakes come
and the mountains crumble into the sea.
NLT.3:
Let the oceans roar and foam.
Let the mountains tremble as the waters surge! Interlude
NLT.4:
A river brings joy to the city of our God,
the sacred home of the Most High.
NLT.5:
God dwells in that city; it cannot be destroyed.
From the very break of day, God will protect it.
NLT.6:
The nations are in chaos,
and their kingdoms crumble!
God’s voice thunders,
and the earth melts!
NLT.7:
The LORD of Heaven’s Armies is here among us;
the God of Israel is our fortress. Interlude
NLT.8:
Come, see the glorious works of the LORD:
See how he brings destruction upon the world.
NLT.9:
He causes wars to end throughout the earth.
He breaks the bow and snaps the spear;
he burns the shields with fire.
NLT.10:
“Be still, and know that I am God!
I will be honored by every nation.
I will be honored throughout the world.”
NLT.11:
The LORD of Heaven’s Armies is here among us;
the God of Israel is our fortress. Interlude

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. By the sons of Korah. According to Alamoth. God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
WEB.2: Therefore we won’t be afraid, though the earth changes, though the mountains are shaken into the heart of the seas;
WEB.3: though its waters roar and are troubled, though the mountains tremble with their swelling. Selah.
WEB.4: There is a river, the streams of which make the city of God glad, the holy place of the tents of the Most High.
WEB.5: God is within her. She shall not be moved. God will help her at dawn.
WEB.6: The nations raged. The kingdoms were moved. He lifted his voice, and the earth melted.
WEB.7: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.
WEB.8: Come, see Yahweh’s works, what desolations he has made in the earth.
WEB.9: He makes wars cease to the end of the earth. He breaks the bow, and shatters the spear. He burns the chariots in the fire.
WEB.10: “Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations. I will be exalted in the earth.”
WEB.11: Yahweh of Armies is with us. The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A song of the sons of Korah. According to . ⓛ [1] God is our refuge and strength,
a helper who is always found
in times of trouble. ⓜ
CSB17.2:
Therefore we will not be afraid,
though the earth trembles
and the mountains topple
into the depths of the seas, ⓝ
CSB17.3:
though its water roars and foams
and the mountains quake with its turmoil. ⓐ Selah
CSB17.4:
There is a river —
its streams delight the city of God,
the holy dwelling place of the Most High. ⓑ
CSB17.5:
God is within her; she will not be toppled.
God will help her when the morning dawns. ⓒ
CSB17.6:
Nations rage, kingdoms topple;
the earth melts when he lifts his voice. ⓓ
CSB17.7:
The LORD of Armies is with us;
the God of Jacob is our stronghold. ⓔ Selah
CSB17.8:
Come, see the works of the LORD,
who brings devastation on the earth. ⓕ
CSB17.9:
He makes wars cease throughout the earth.
He shatters bows and cuts spears to pieces;
he sets wagons ablaze. ⓖ
CSB17.10:
“Stop your fighting, and know that I am God,
exalted among the nations, exalted on the earth.” ⓗ
CSB17.11:
The LORD of Armies is with us;
the God of Jacob is our stronghold. ⓘ Selah

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah, set to soprano voices. A Song. God is our refuge and strength [mighty and impenetrable],
A very present and well-proved help in trouble.
AMP.2:
Therefore we will not fear, though the earth should change
And though the mountains be shaken and slip into the heart of the seas,
AMP.3:
Though its waters roar and foam,
Though the mountains tremble at its roaring. Selah.
AMP.4:
There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God,
The holy dwelling places of the Most High.
AMP.5:
God is in the midst of her [His city], she will not be moved;
God will help her when the morning dawns.
AMP.6:
The nations made an uproar, the kingdoms tottered and were moved;
He raised His voice, the earth melted.
AMP.7:
The LORD of hosts is with us;
The God of Jacob is our stronghold [our refuge, our high tower]. Selah.
AMP.8:
Come, behold the works of the LORD,
Who has wrought desolations and wonders in the earth.
AMP.9:
He makes wars to cease to the end of the earth;
He breaks the bow into pieces and snaps the spear in two;
He burns the chariots with fire.
AMP.10:
"Be still and know (recognize, understand) that I am God.
I will be exalted among the nations! I will be exalted in the earth."
AMP.11:
The LORD of hosts is with us;
The God of Jacob is our stronghold [our refuge, our high tower]. Selah.

MSG.1:
A song of the sons of Korah
God is a safe place to hide,
ready to help when we need him.
MSG.2:
We stand fearless at the cliff-edge of doom,
courageous in seastorm and earthquake,
MSG.3:
Before the rush and roar of oceans,
the tremors that shift mountains.
Jacob-wrestling God fights for us,
God of angel armies protects us.
MSG.4:
River fountains splash joy, cooling God's city,
this sacred haunt of the Most High.
MSG.5:
God lives here, the streets are safe,
God at your service from crack of dawn.
MSG.6:
Godless nations rant and rave, kings and kingdoms threaten,
but Earth does anything he says.
MSG.7:
Jacob-wrestling God fights for us,
God of angel armies protects us.
MSG.8:
Attention, all! See the marvels of God!
He plants flowers and trees all over the earth,
MSG.9:
Bans war from pole to pole,
breaks all the weapons across his knee.
MSG.10:
"Step out of the traffic! Take a long,
loving look at me, your High God,
above politics, above everything."
MSG.11:
Jacob-wrestling God fights for us,
God of angel armies protects us.

NIV.1:
Psalm 46
For the director of music. Of the Sons of Korah. According to alamoth. A song.
God is our refuge and strength,
an ever-present help in trouble.
NIV.2:
Therefore we will not fear, though the earth give way
and the mountains fall into the heart of the sea,
NIV.3:
though its waters roar and foam
and the mountains quake with their surging. Selah
NIV.4:
There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God,
the holy place where the Most High dwells.
NIV.5:
God is within her, she will not fall;
God will help her at break of day.
NIV.6:
Nations are in uproar, kingdoms fall;
he lifts his voice, the earth melts.
NIV.7:
The Lord Almighty is with us;
the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah
NIV.8:
Come and see the works of the Lord,
the desolations he has brought on the earth.
NIV.9:
He makes wars cease to the ends of the earth;
he breaks the bow and shatters the spear,
he burns the shields with fire.
NIV.10:
“Be still, and know that I am God;
I will be exalted among the nations,
I will be exalted in the earth.”
NIV.11:
The Lord Almighty is with us;
the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah

NRSVCE.1:
God is our refuge and strength,
a very present help in trouble.
NRSVCE.2:
Therefore we will not fear, though the earth should change,
though the mountains shake in the heart of the sea;
NRSVCE.3:
though its waters roar and foam,
though the mountains tremble with its tumult. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
There is a river whose streams make glad the city of God,
the holy habitation of the Most High.
NRSVCE.5:
God is in the midst of the city; it shall not be moved;
God will help it when the morning dawns.
NRSVCE.6:
The nations are in an uproar, the kingdoms totter;
he utters his voice, the earth melts.
NRSVCE.7:
The LORD of hosts is with us;
the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah
NRSVCE.8:
Come, behold the works of the LORD;
see what desolations he has brought on the earth.
NRSVCE.9:
He makes wars cease to the end of the earth;
he breaks the bow, and shatters the spear;
he burns the shields with fire.
NRSVCE.10:
“Be still, and know that I am God!
I am exalted among the nations,
I am exalted in the earth.”
NRSVCE.11:
The LORD of hosts is with us;
the God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah

NET.1: God is our strong refuge; he is truly our helper in times of trouble.
NET.2: For this reason we do not fear when the earth shakes, and the mountains tumble into the depths of the sea,
NET.3: when its waves crash and foam, and the mountains shake before the surging sea. (Selah)
NET.4: The river’s channels bring joy to the city of God, the special, holy dwelling place of the sovereign One.
NET.5: God lives within it, it cannot be moved. God rescues it at the break of dawn.
NET.6: Nations are in uproar, kingdoms are overthrown. God gives a shout, the earth dissolves.
NET.7: The LORD who commands armies is on our side! The God of Jacob is our protector! (Selah)
NET.8: Come! Witness the exploits of the LORD, who brings devastation to the earth!
NET.9: He brings an end to wars throughout the earth; he shatters the bow and breaks the spear; he burns the shields with fire.
NET.10: He says, “Stop your striving and recognize that I am God! I will be exalted over the nations! I will be exalted over the earth!”
NET.11: The LORD who commands armies is on our side! The God of Jacob is our protector! (Selah)
